source: chacha20-poly1305-stream64k chunk-layout vectors under a pinned payload_key: per-chunk nonce uint88_be(counter) || final_flag, empty per-chunk AAD, 65536-byte plaintext chunks, 16-byte tags. Positives pin the empty / one-byte / exact-chunk / boundary-crossing layouts; negatives apply the named byte transforms (flip_byte / truncate_to / append_hex / remove, in order) to a positive's ciphertext and MUST fail decryption as TAMPERED_CIPHERTEXT. Tag-flip coverage spans both a final and a non-final chunk; the truncation shapes cover a removed final chunk, a tail too short to be a chunk, and a blob below the single-tag floor; forged-empty-final-chunk-after-data carries a correctly authenticated zero-length final chunk that MUST still be rejected, because an empty final chunk encodes only the empty plaintext.
